text:
*[Bug 19001] New: utils:::str.default() does not omit "List of ..." when called via NextMethod() by a registered S3 method*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19001>

            Bug ID: 19001
           Summary: utils:::str.default() does not omit "List of ..." when
                    called via NextMethod() by a registered S3 method
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: x86_64/x64/amd64 (64-bit)
                OS: Linux-Ubuntu
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Misc
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:ian.farm@maine.edu|ian.farm@maine.edu>

The output of `utils:::str.default()`, when called via `NextMethod()`,
is different when the calling method is registered with the utils S3
methods table than when the calling method is defined in the global
environment. Specifically, when called on a list object by a registered
method, `str.default()` includes a header ("List of ...") and the class
attribute; but when called by a method in the global environment, it
does not.

*Steps to reproduce*

Case where the calling method is defined in global environment:

    str.someclass <- function(object, ...) {
      cat("<someclass> of length ", length(object), "\n")
      NextMethod()
    }

    x <- structure(list(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3), class = "someclass")
    str(x)

    rm("str.someclass") # clean up

    ## <someclass> of length  3 
    ##  $ a: num 1
    ##  $ b: num 2
    ##  $ c: num 3

Compare to the case where the method is registered with the S3 methods
table:

    .S3method("str", "someclass", 
      function(object, ...) {
        cat("<someclass> of length ", length(object), "\n")
        NextMethod()
      }
    )

    x <- structure(list(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3), class = "someclass")
    str(x)

    rm("str.someclass", envir = loadNamespace("utils")$.__S3MethodsTable__.) #
clean up

    ## <someclass> of length  3 
    ## List of 3
    ##  $ a: num 1
    ##  $ b: num 2
    ##  $ c: num 3
    ##  - attr(*, "class")= chr "someclass"

The result is the same if the method is registered by a package:

    # Write example package source to a temporary directory
    tmp <- tempfile()
    dir.create(tmp)
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "somepackage"))
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "R"))
    cat('Package: somepackage
    Version: 0.0.0
    ', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "DESCRIPTION"))
    cat('str.someclass <- function(object, ...) {
      cat("<someclass> of length ", length(object), "\n")
      NextMethod()
    }', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "R", "someclass.R"))
    cat('S3method(utils::str, someclass)
    ', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "NAMESPACE"))
    # Install the example package in a temporary library
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "library"))
    system2("R", c("CMD", "INSTALL", 
      paste0("--library=", file.path(tmp, "library")), 
      file.path(tmp, "somepackage")), stdout = FALSE, stderr = FALSE)

    library("somepackage", lib.loc = file.path(tmp, "library"), quietly = TRUE)

    x <- structure(list(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3), class = "someclass")
    str(x)

    # clean up
    unloadNamespace("somepackage")
    rm("str.someclass", envir = loadNamespace("utils")$.__S3MethodsTable__.)
    unlink(tmp, recursive = TRUE)

    ## <someclass> of length  3 
    ## List of 3
    ##  $ a: num 1
    ##  $ b: num 2
    ##  $ c: num 3
    ##  - attr(*, "class")= chr "someclass"

*Diagnosis*

`str.default()` uses the following logic to check whether it is a
'NextMethod' (str.R ll. 347-54)

    if(no.list || (has.class &&
                   any(sapply(paste0("str.", cl),
                                #use sys.function(.) ..
                              function(ob)exists(ob, mode= "function",
                                                 inherits= TRUE))))) {

    # Write example package source to a temporary directory
    tmp <- tempfile()
    dir.create(tmp)
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "somepackage"))
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "R"))

    Version: 0.0.0
    ', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "DESCRIPTION"))
    cat('str.someclass <- function(object, ...) {
      cat("<someclass> of length ", length(object), "\n")
      NextMethod()
    }', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "R", "someclass.R"))
    cat('S3method(utils::str, someclass)
    ', file = file.path(tmp, "somepackage", "NAMESPACE"))
    # Install the example package in a temporary library
    dir.create(file.path(tmp, "library"))
    system2("R", c("CMD", "INSTALL", 
      paste0("--library=", file.path(tmp, "library")), 
      file.path(tmp, "somepackage")), stdout = FALSE, stderr = FALSE)

    library("somepackage", lib.loc = file.path(tmp, "library"), quietly = TRUE)

    x <- structure(list(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3), class = "someclass")
    str(x)

    # clean up
    unloadNamespace("somepackage")
    rm("str.someclass", envir = loadNamespace("utils")$.__S3MethodsTable__.)
    unlink(tmp, recursive = TRUE)

    ## <someclass> of length  3 
    ## List of 3
    ##  $ a: num 1
    ##  $ b: num 2
    ##  $ c: num 3
    ##  - attr(*, "class")= chr "someclass"

uses the following logic to check whether it is a
'NextMethod' (str.R ll. 347-54)

    if(no.list || (has.class &&
                   any(sapply(paste0("str.", cl),
                                #use sys.function(.) ..
                              function(ob)exists(ob, mode= "function",
                                                 inherits= TRUE))))) {

text:
## str.default is a 'NextMethod' : omit the 'List of ..'
        std.attr <- c(std.attr, "class", if(is.d.f) "row.names")
    }

When `str.someclass()` is registered in an S3 methods table, `exists()`
does not find it, and the condition evaluates to `FALSE` despite
`str.default()` having been called via `NextMethod()`. It does find
methods that are defined in the global environment, are exported to a
package environment, or are part of the utils namespace (e.g.,
`str.data.frame()`).

*Proposed solution*

Based on the comment in the above snippet from str.R, I might suggest
replacing the condition of the above if statement with something along
the lines of:

    no.list || identical(sys.function(-1), NextMethod)

Or possibly:

    no.list || (exists(".Call") && !is.null(.Call))

Alternatively, if this is expected behavior, it could be noted in
`?str`\#Note.

        std.attr <- c(std.attr, "class", if(is.d.f) "row.names")
    }

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18994>

Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) changed:

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |michaelchirico4@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3606
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3606&action=edit>
Fix off-by-one errors in CONTEXTSTACK

I deployed Gemini on this since it's pretty firmly in LLM capability wheelhouse
-- patch attached, which also found 4 other errors similar to the one reported.

Testing on GitHub: <https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/pull/232>

I confirmed each of them individually result in a segfault before the gram.c
change is applied, and that there is no segfault afterwards.

Fix off-by-one errors in CONTEXTSTACK

I deployed Gemini on this since it's pretty firmly in LLM capability wheelhouse
-- patch attached, which also found 4 other errors similar to the one reported.

Testing on GitHub:

I confirmed each of them individually result in a segfault before the gram.c
change is applied, and that there is no segfault afterwards.

*[Bug 18749] sort_by() all columns*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18749>

Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |michaelchirico4@gmail.com

--- Comment #3 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
I would have expected `~.` not to work due to `.` being a valid column name,
but actually `sort_by()` is already not supported in that case:

```
x = data.frame(a = 1:10, b = 10:1, `.` = rnorm(10))
sort_by(x, ~.)
# Error in terms.formula(formula) : '.' in formula and no 'data' argument
```

text:
*[Bug 19002] New: Referent of 'it' in R CMD check NOTE about LinkingTo is ambiguous*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19002>

            Bug ID: 19002
           Summary: Referent of 'it' in R CMD check NOTE about LinkingTo
                    is ambiguous
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Misc
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3607
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3607&action=edit>
Restructure sentence to avoid 'it'

When I encoutered this NOTE in `R CMD check` for package `A` with `LinkingTo:
B`, I assumed it was referring to an issue _with package `A`_.

After some wasted time debugging, eventually I realized it was referring to an
issue _with package `B`_.

In light of this, I am hoping we can just reword the sentence to avoid this
ambiguity.
